center.name: 西印度群岛
center.summary: 西印度群岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联23种鸟类，包括草黃樹鴨、美洲紅鸛、普通珠雞等23种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: They are migratory, wintering in Central America, the West Indies, and northern South America, and are very rare vagrants to western Europe

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Fish & Wildlife Service|access-date=19 January 2024}} | genus = Pterodroma | species = hasitata | authority = (Kuhl, 1820) | range_map = Pterodroma hasitata map.svg }} The black-capped petrel (Pterodroma hasitata), also known as the diablotín, is a small seabird native to the West Indies in the genus Pterodroma

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in the West Indies except Cuba, on Tobago but not Trinidad, and along the northern coasts of Colombia and Venezuela

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, wintering in the West Indies

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |location= Ithaca |publisher= Cornell Lab of Ornithology |url=https://bna.birds.cornell.edu/bna/species/448}} wintering in the southern parts of the breeding range, Central America and the West Indies

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Green-rumped parrotlets are found in tropical South America, from the Caribbean regions of Colombia, Venezuela and Trinidad south and east to the Guianas and Brazil on the lower Amazon River. It has been introduced in Jamaica, Curaçao, Barbados and Tobago, and was not recorded on Trinidad prior to 1916. Along with the lilac-tailed parrotlet (Touit batavicus), they are the only parrotlet species to occur in the West Indies. Green-rumped parrotlets are fairly common in open, semi-arid habitat and are found residing in dry scrubland, deciduous woodland, gallery forest, farmland, forest edges, and deforested areas throughout their range

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, wintering in Central America and the West Indies

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: La Sagra's flycatcher (Myiarchus sagrae) is a passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Cuba, the Bahamas, and Grand Cayman in the West Indies and as a vagrant to Alabama and Florida.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The loggerhead kingbird is found in throughout the northern West Indies, in the Bahamas, Cayman Islands, Cuba, Isla de la Juventud, Hispaniola (the Dominican Republic and Haiti), Jamaica and Puerto Rico. It very rarely occurs in southern Florida as a vagrant.{{cite web |url=https://www.birds-of-north-america.net/Loggerhead_Kingbird.html#:~:text=The%20Loggerhead%20Kingbird%20is%20a,but%20displays%20a%20bulkier%20body

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: motacilla | synonyms = Seiurus motacilla }} thumb|Singing from a perch near an audible brook, Glastonbury, CT thumb The Louisiana waterthrush (Parkesia motacilla) is a New World warbler, that breeds in eastern North America and winters in the West Indies and Central America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It inhabits the Centre Hills and South Soufriere Hills Important Bird Areas on the island of Montserrat in the Lesser Antilles of the West Indies, and is the national bird of this British territory

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|right|At Galveston, Texas during spring migration thumb|Resting during fall migration in New York thumb|right|At High Island, Texas This species is migratory, wintering in southern Florida, northern Central America, the West Indies and most of the Lesser Antilles

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in the northern part of North America, in Alaska, Canada, and the northern United States; it winters in Florida, Central America, the West Indies, Venezuela, Colombia, and Ecuador.{{Cite journal |last1=Smith |first1=Joseph A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: savana, is found in central and southern Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, and Argentina (south to the Río Negro), and overwinters in Amazonia, a large portion of northern South America (i.e., within the Orinoco River Basin), and Trinidad and Tobago, occasionally appearing in the West Indies.{{Cite journal|last=Rosenberg|first=Gary H.|date=July 2010|title=Field Guide to the Songbirds of South America.— Robert S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occasionally breeds further south in the US, and vagrants are sometimes found in the Arctic Circle, the northern Pacific, Greenland, and Europe. The wintering range is from California and southwestern Arizona in the west and southeastern Virginia in the east south along the Gulf Coast to the West Indies, Panama, and the northwestern South American coast. While migrating, this swallow often uses stop-over sites, spending an average of 57 days at these areas during autumn

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to Africa, mainly south of the Sahara, and has been widely introduced, as a domesticated species, into the West Indies, North America, Colombia, Brazil,{{cite web |title=Tô-fraco, galinha d'angola, capota ou cocá?

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The pearly-eyed thrasher has a somewhat disjunct distribution throughout the West Indies: it is found in the Bahamas, Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, the Turks and Caicos Islands, the eastern Dominican Republic (as well as Isla Beata), many of the Lesser Antilles (except Barbados and Grenada, where it is extirpated), and Bonaire. Its main habitat is bushes and trees in mountain forests and coffee plantations

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |date=23 April 2021 |title=Decline of novel ecosystems used by endangered species: the case of piping plovers, least terns, and aggregate mines |journal=Ecosphere |volume=12 |issue=4 |pages=e03474 |language=en |doi=10.1002/ecs2.3474 |bibcode=2021Ecosp..12E3474J |s2cid=236754336 |doi-access=free }} Piping plovers migrate from their northern range in the summer to the south in the winter months, migrating to the Gulf of Mexico, the southern Atlantic coast of the United States and the Caribbean, including The Bahamas. They have also been recorded across Cuba, with rarer occurrences elsewhere throughout the West Indies, and even Ecuador and Venezuela.{{cite journal |last1=Sharpe |first1=Christopher |title=First records of Piping Plover Charadrius melodus for Venezuela, with a revision of its non-breeding distribution

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The American flamingo (Phoenicopterus ruber) is a large species of flamingo native to the West Indies, northern South America (including the Galápagos Islands) and the Yucatán Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The fulvous whistling duck or fulvous tree duck (Dendrocygna bicolor) is a species of whistling duck that breeds across the world's tropical regions in much of Mexico and South America, the West Indies, the southern United States, sub-Saharan Africa and the Indian subcontinent

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: These birds winter in the eastern United States, West Indies and Central America

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in northern and eastern North America and winters in Florida, Central America, and the West Indies down to Peru

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The population of the species spends the spring and summer in its breeding range in the Great Lakes region of Canada (Ontario) and the United States (Wisconsin and Michigan, especially in the northeastern Lower Peninsula), and winters in the West Indies
